 {"id":"bd-kzda","title":"Implement conditional bond type for mol bond","description":"The mol bond command accepts 'conditional' as a bond type but doesn't implement any conditional-specific behavior. It currently behaves identically to 'parallel'.

**Expected behavior:**
Conditional bonds should mean 'B runs only if A fails' per the help text (mol.go:318).

**Implementation needed:**
- Add failure-condition dependency handling
- Possibly new dependency type or status-based blocking
- Update bondProtoProto, bondProtoMol, bondMolMol to handle conditional

**Alternative:**
Remove 'conditional' from valid bond types until implemented.

This is new functionality, not a regression.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T10:23:01.966367-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:33:25.734264-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T01:33:25.734264-08:00"}

Enables activity feed TUI to show worker state without multiple round trips.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T16:26:36.248654-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T16:40:59.772138-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T16:40:59.772138-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-l6yk","title":"bd mol burn should work on mols, not just wisps","description":"Currently `bd mol burn` only works on wisps (Ephemeral=true):

```
$ bd mol burn bd-mol-p7i
Error: molecule bd-mol-p7i is not a wisp (Ephemeral=false)
Hint: mol burn only works with wisp molecules
      Use 'bd delete' to remove non-wisp issues
```

The name 'burn' implies destruction - it should work on any molecule, not just wisps. When called on a regular mol, it should convert to `bd delete --cascade`.

**Proposed behavior:**
- If wisp: current behavior (delete without tombstones)
- If mol: `bd delete <id> --cascade` (delete with tombstones)

This makes the metaphor consistent: burn = destroy a molecule, regardless of phase.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T17:05:08.115592-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-29T17:05:08.115592-08:00"}

Hint: mol burn only works with wisp molecules
      Use 'bd delete' to remove non-wisp issues
```

The name 'burn' implies destruction - it should work on any molecule, not just wisps. When called on a regular mol, it should convert to `bd delete --cascade`.

- If mol: `bd delete <id> --cascade` (delete with tombstones)

 {"id":"bd-l7y3","title":"bd mol bond --pour should set Wisp=false","description":"In mol_bond.go bondProtoMol(), opts.Wisp is hardcoded to true (line 392). This ignores the --pour flag. When user specifies --pour to make an issue persistent, the Wisp field should be false so the issue is not marked for bulk deletion.

Current behavior:
- --pour flag correctly selects regular storage (not wisp storage)
- But opts.Wisp=true means spawned issues are still marked for cleanup when closed

Expected behavior:
- --pour should set Wisp=false so persistent issues are not auto-cleaned

Comparison with mol_spawn.go (line 204):
  wisp := !pour  // Correctly respects --pour flag
  result, err := spawnMolecule(ctx, store, subgraph, vars, assignee, actor, wisp)

Fix: Pass pour flag to bondProtoMol and set opts.Wisp = !pour","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T15:15:00.562346-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T15:25:22.53144-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T15:25:22.53144-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-ldb0","title":"Rename ephemeral → wisp throughout codebase","description":"## The Change

Rename 'ephemeral' to 'wisp' throughout the beads codebase.

## Why

**Ephemeral** is:
- 4 syllables (too long)
- Greek/academic (doesn't match bond/burn/squash)
- Overused in tech (K8s, networking, storage)
- Passive/descriptive

**Wisp** is:
- 1 syllable (matches bond/burn/squash)
- Evocative - you can SEE a wisp
- Steam engine metaphor - Gas Town is engines, steam wisps rise and dissipate
- Will-o'-the-wisp - transient spirits that guide then vanish
- Unique - nobody else uses it

## The Steam Engine Metaphor

```
Engine does work → generates steam
Steam wisps rise → execution trace
Steam condenses → digest (distillate)
Steam dissipates → cleaned up (burned)
```

## Full Vocabulary

| Term | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| bond | Attach proto to work (creates wisps) |
| wisp | Temporary execution step |
| squash | Condense wisps into digest |
| burn | Destroy wisps without record |
| digest | Permanent condensed record |

## Changes Required

### Code
- `Ephemeral bool` → `Wisp bool` in types/issue.go
- `--ephemeral` flag → remove (wisp is default)
- `--persistent` flag → keep as opt-out
- `bd cleanup --ephemeral` → `bd cleanup --wisps`
- Update all references in mol_*.go files

### Docs
- Update all documentation
- Update CLAUDE.md examples
- Update CLI help text

### Database Migration
- Add migration to rename field (or keep internal name, just change API)

## Example Usage After

```bash
bd mol bond mol-polecat-work   # Creates wisps (default)
bd mol bond mol-xxx --persistent  # Creates permanent issues
bd mol squash bd-xxx           # Condenses wisps → digest
bd cleanup --wisps             # Clean old wisps
bd list --wisps                # Show wisp issues
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:44:41.576068-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T00:32:31.153738-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T00:32:31.153738-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-lfak","title":"bd preflight: PR readiness checks for contributors","description":"## Vision

Encode project-specific institutional knowledge into executable checks. CONTRIBUTING.md is documentation that's read once and forgotten; `bd preflight` is documentation that runs at exactly the right moment.

## Problem Statement

Contributors face a "last mile" problem - they do the work but stumble on project-specific gotchas at PR time:
- Nix vendorHash gets stale when go.sum changes
- Beads artifacts leak into PRs (see bd-umbf for namespace solution)
- Version mismatches between version.go and default.nix
- Tests/lint not run locally before pushing
- Other project-specific checks that only surface when CI fails

These are too obscure to remember, exist in docs nobody reads end-to-end, and waste CI round-trips.

## Why beads?

Beads already has a foothold in the contributor workflow. It knows:
- Git state (staged files, branch, dirty status)
- Project structure
- The specific issue being worked on
- Project-specific configuration

## Proposed Interface

### Tier 1: Checklist Mode (v1)

    $ bd preflight
    PR Readiness Checklist:

    [ ] Tests pass: go test -short ./...
    [ ] Lint passes: golangci-lint run ./...
    [ ] No beads pollution: check .beads/issues.jsonl diff
    [ ] Nix hash current: go.sum unchanged or vendorHash updated
    [ ] Version sync: version.go matches default.nix

    Run 'bd preflight --check' to validate automatically.

### Tier 2: Check Mode (v2)

    $ bd preflight --check
    ✓ Tests pass
    ✓ Lint passes
    ⚠ Beads pollution: 3 issues in diff - are these project issues or personal?
    ✗ Nix hash stale: go.sum changed, vendorHash needs update
      Fix: sha256-KRR6dXzsSw8OmEHGBEVDBOoIgfoZ2p0541T9ayjGHlI=
    ✓ Version sync

    1 error, 1 warning. Run 'bd preflight --fix' to auto-fix where possible.

### Tier 3: Fix Mode (v3)

    $ bd preflight --fix
    ✓ Updated vendorHash in default.nix
    ⚠ Cannot auto-fix beads pollution - manual review needed

## Checks to Implement

| Check | Description | Auto-fixable |
|-------|-------------|--------------|
| tests | Run go test -short ./... | No |
| lint | Run golangci-lint | Partial (gofmt) |
| beads-pollution | Detect personal issues in diff | No (see bd-umbf) |
| nix-hash | Detect stale vendorHash | Yes (if nix available) |
| version-sync | version.go matches default.nix | Yes |
| no-debug | No TODO/FIXME/console.log | Warn only |
| clean-stage | No unintended files staged | Warn only |

## Future: Configuration

Make checks configurable per-project via .beads/preflight.yaml:

    preflight:
      checks:
        - name: tests
          run: go test -short ./...
          required: true
        - name: no-secrets
          pattern: "**/*.env"
          staged: deny
        - name: custom-check
          run: ./scripts/validate.sh

This lets any project using beads define their own preflight checks.

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Static Checklist
- Implement bd preflight with hardcoded checklist for beads
- No execution, just prints what to check
- Update CONTRIBUTING.md to reference it

### Phase 2: Automated Checks
- Implement bd preflight --check
- Run tests, lint, detect stale hashes
- Clear pass/fail/warn output

### Phase 3: Auto-fix
- Implement bd preflight --fix
- Fix vendorHash, version sync
- Integrate with bd-umbf solution for pollution

### Phase 4: Configuration
- .beads/preflight.yaml support
- Make it useful for other projects using beads
- Plugin/hook system for custom checks

## Dependencies

- bd-umbf: Namespace isolation for beads pollution (blocking for full solution)

## Success Metrics

- Fewer CI failures on first PR push
- Reduced "fix nix hash" commits
- Contributors report preflight caught issues before CI","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:01:39.587078-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:01:39.587078-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lfak","depends_on_id":"bd-umbf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:01:46.059901-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-m964","title":"Consider FTS5 for text search at scale","description":"SearchIssues uses LIKE patterns for text search which can't use indexes.

**Current query (queries.go:1475-1477):**
```sql
(title LIKE ? OR description LIKE ? OR id LIKE ?)
```

**Problem:** Full table scan on every text search. At 100K+ issues, this becomes slow.

**SQLite FTS5 solution:**
```sql
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE issues_fts USING fts5(
  id, title, description, design, notes,
  content='issues',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER issues_ai AFTER INSERT ON issues BEGIN
  INSERT INTO issues_fts(rowid, id, title, description, design, notes)
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.description, new.design, new.notes);
END;
-- (similar for UPDATE, DELETE)
```

**Trade-offs:**
- Database size increase (~30-50% for text content)
- Additional write overhead (trigger execution)
- Better search capabilities (ranking, phrase search)

**Decision needed:** Is full-text search a priority feature? Current LIKE search may be acceptable for most use cases.

**Benchmark first:** Measure SearchIssues at 100K scale before implementing.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466121-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466121-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m964","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466764-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mol-0qm","title":"mol-beads-release","description":"attached_args: Release v0.38.0 - execute each step in order, verify before proceeding to next

Release checklist for beads version 0.38.0.

This molecule ensures all release steps are completed properly.
Variable: 0.38.0 - target version (e.g., 0.35.0)

## Step: update-release-notes
Update cmd/bd/info.go with release notes for 0.38.0.

Add a new VersionChange entry at the top of versionChanges slice:
```go
{
    Version: "0.38.0",
    Date:    "YYYY-MM-DD",
    Changes: []string{
        "NEW: Feature description",
        "FIX: Bug fix description",
        "IMPROVED: Enhancement description",
    },
},
```

Run `git log --oneline v<previous>..HEAD` to see what changed.

## Step: update-changelog
Update CHANGELOG.md with detailed release notes.

Add a new section after [Unreleased]:
```markdown
## [0.38.0] - YYYY-MM-DD

### Added
- **Feature name** (issue-id) - Description

### Changed
- **Change description** (issue-id)

### Fixed
- **Bug fix** (issue-id) - Description
```

Sort by importance, not chronologically.
Needs: update-release-notes

## Step: bump-version
Run the version bump script.

```bash
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0
```

This updates version in all files:
- cmd/bd/version.go
- .claude-plugin/*.json
- integrations/beads-mcp/pyproject.toml
- npm-package/package.json
- Hook templates

Needs: update-changelog

## Step: run-tests
Run tests and verify lint passes.

```bash
go test -short ./...
```

CI will run full lint, but fix any obvious issues first.
Needs: bump-version

## Step: commit-release
Commit the release changes.

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: bump version to v0.38.0"
```

Needs: run-tests

## Step: push-and-tag
Push commit and create release tag.

```bash
git push origin main
git tag v0.38.0
git push origin v0.38.0
```

This triggers GitHub Actions release workflow.
Needs: commit-release

## Step: wait-for-ci
Wait for GitHub Actions to complete.

Monitor: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/actions

CI will:
- Build binaries via GoReleaser
- Create GitHub Release with assets
- Publish to npm (@beads/bd)
- Publish to PyPI (beads-mcp)
- Update Homebrew tap

Wait until all jobs succeed (~5-10 min).
Needs: push-and-tag

## Step: verify-release
Verify the release is complete.

```bash
# Check GitHub release
gh release view v0.38.0

# Check Homebrew
brew update && brew info steveyegge/beads/bd

# Check npm
npm view @beads/bd version

# Check PyPI
pip index versions beads-mcp
```

Needs: wait-for-ci

## Step: update-local
Update local installations.

```bash
# Upgrade Homebrew
brew upgrade steveyegge/beads/bd

# Or install from source
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --install

# Install MCP locally
pip install -e integrations/beads-mcp

# Restart daemons
pkill -f "bd daemon" || true
```

Verify: `bd --version` shows 0.38.0
Needs: verify-release

## Step: manual-publish
(Optional) Manual publish if CI failed.

```bash
# npm (requires npm login)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --publish-npm

# PyPI (requires TWINE credentials)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --publish-pypi

# Or both
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --publish-all
```

Only needed if CI publishing failed.

+{"id":"bd-mol-4xo","title":"beads-release","description":"Beads release workflow - from version bump to verified release.

This formula orchestrates a complete release cycle:
1. Preflight checks (clean git, up to date)
2. Documentation updates (CHANGELOG, info.go)
3. Version bump (all components)
4. Git operations (commit, tag, push)
5. CI verification (GitHub Actions)
6. Artifact verification (GitHub, npm, PyPI)
7. Local installation update
8. Daemon restart

## Usage

```bash
bd wisp create beads-release --var version=0.37.0
```

Or assign to a polecat:
```bash
gt sling beads/polecats/p1 --formula beads-release --var version=0.37.0
```","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T17:09:12.46423-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T17:09:32.480437-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-29T17:09:32.480437-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"epic"}

 {"id":"bd-mol-xga","title":"mol-beads-release","description":"Release checklist for beads version 0.37.0.

This molecule ensures all release steps are completed properly.
Variable: 0.37.0 - target version (e.g., 0.35.0)

## Step: update-release-notes
Update cmd/bd/info.go with release notes for 0.37.0.

Add a new VersionChange entry at the top of versionChanges slice:
```go
{
    Version: "0.37.0",
    Date:    "YYYY-MM-DD",
    Changes: []string{
        "NEW: Feature description",
        "FIX: Bug fix description",
        "IMPROVED: Enhancement description",
    },
},
```

Run `git log --oneline v<previous>..HEAD` to see what changed.

## Step: update-changelog
Update CHANGELOG.md with detailed release notes.

Add a new section after [Unreleased]:
```markdown
## [0.37.0] - YYYY-MM-DD

### Added
- **Feature name** (issue-id) - Description

### Changed
- **Change description** (issue-id)

### Fixed
- **Bug fix** (issue-id) - Description
```

Sort by importance, not chronologically.
Needs: update-release-notes

## Step: bump-version
Run the version bump script.

```bash
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0
```

This updates version in all files:
- cmd/bd/version.go
- .claude-plugin/*.json
- integrations/beads-mcp/pyproject.toml
- npm-package/package.json
- Hook templates

Needs: update-changelog

## Step: run-tests
Run tests and verify lint passes.

```bash
go test -short ./...
```

CI will run full lint, but fix any obvious issues first.
Needs: bump-version

## Step: commit-release
Commit the release changes.

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: bump version to v0.37.0"
```

Needs: run-tests

## Step: push-and-tag
Push commit and create release tag.

```bash
git push origin main
git tag v0.37.0
git push origin v0.37.0
```

This triggers GitHub Actions release workflow.
Needs: commit-release

## Step: wait-for-ci
Wait for GitHub Actions to complete.

Monitor: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/actions

CI will:
- Build binaries via GoReleaser
- Create GitHub Release with assets
- Publish to npm (@beads/bd)
- Publish to PyPI (beads-mcp)
- Update Homebrew tap

Wait until all jobs succeed (~5-10 min).
Needs: push-and-tag

## Step: verify-release
Verify the release is complete.

```bash
# Check GitHub release
gh release view v0.37.0

# Check Homebrew
brew update && brew info steveyegge/beads/bd

# Check npm
npm view @beads/bd version

# Check PyPI
pip index versions beads-mcp
```

Needs: wait-for-ci

## Step: update-local
Update local installations.

```bash
# Upgrade Homebrew
brew upgrade steveyegge/beads/bd

# Or install from source
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --install

# Install MCP locally
pip install -e integrations/beads-mcp

# Restart daemons
pkill -f "bd daemon" || true
```

Verify: `bd --version` shows 0.37.0
Needs: verify-release

## Step: manual-publish
(Optional) Manual publish if CI failed.

```bash
# npm (requires npm login)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --publish-npm

# PyPI (requires TWINE credentials)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --publish-pypi

# Or both
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.37.0 --publish-all
```

Only needed if CI publishing failed.
